question: Did the review adhere to pre-defined objectives and eligibility criteria?
answers: nan

question: Were the eligibility crieria appropriate for the review question?
answers:
The objective of this meta-analysis was to systematically review randomized
controlled trials (RCTs) of VTE prophylaxis in medically ill patients to determine whether there is a
difference in efficacy and safety profiles between UFH, LMWH (or fondaparinux), and placebo.

studies were included in the analysis if
they included medically ill patients with risk factors
for VTE who had been followed up for 7 to 21 days.
We clustered dalteparin, enoxaparin, nadroparin, and
fondaparinux together in the LMWH/fondaparinux
group. Fondaparinux was included with LMWH agents
for practicality since fondaparinux has been regarded
as an "ultra-LMWH" and because the pharmacokinetic
and pharmacodynamic properties of fondaparinux
are similar to other LMWH agents, 19 although the
US Food and Drug Administration has recently classified
it as a selective factor Xa inhibitor. 2deg,21 Efficacy
end points of any VTE, DVT, or fatal or nonfatal
PE (considered thrombotic events) were selected for
comparison, as well as tolerability end points including
major or minor bleeding, fatal bleeding, and VTErelated
death. We used the diagnostic criteria for medically
ill, VTE, DVT, PE, major bleeding, and minor
bleeding provided by each study. Studies failing to report
drug doses or excluding end points of VTE, fatal
or nonfatal PE, or bleeding were excluded from the
analysis.

answers:

question: Were all restrictions in eligibility crieria based on study characteristics appropriate (e.g. date, sample size, study quality, outcomes measured)? If yes, indicate which study characteristic was an inclusion/exclusion criteria
answers:
Studies failing to report drug doses or excluding end points of VTE, fatal or nonfatal PE, or bleeding were excluded from the analysis.

We only included RCTs with a corresponding Jadad score of 3 or greater (maximum score, 5). Discrepancies in Jadad score assignment and inclusion criteria were resolved through consensus and discussion.

The 5 excluded trials included patients who failed to achieve a minimum Jadad score of 3, mainly due to lack
of randomization. 5,16,17,34,35 Although patients who have experienced stroke are regarded as medically ill, trials that comprised solely stroke patients were excluded as a recently published meta-analysis addresses VTE prophylaxis in this population, and the objective of the current study was to examine an unselected medically ill population as seen in routine clinical practice.

question: Were any restrictions in eligibility criteria based on sources of info appropriate (e.g. publication status or format, language, availability of data)?
answers:
We systematically searched MEDLINE, EMBASE,
and the Cochrane Controlled Trials Registry from
January 1981 through September 2007 for Englishlanguage
RCTs

answers: We searched references of included articles and key review papers for additional studies

question: Were the terms and structure of the search strategy likely to retrieve as many eligible studies as possible?
answers:
using the following
terms: dalteparin, enoxaparin, fondaparinux,
nadroparin, and heparin

question: Were search strategy restrictions based on date, publication format, or language appropriate?
answers:
We systematically searched MEDLINE, EMBASE,
and the Cochrane Controlled Trials Registry from
January 1981 through September 2007 for English language RCTs evaluating UFH, LMWH, or fondaparinux for the reduction of in-hospital VTE in the unselected medically ill population using the following terms: dalteparin, enoxaparin, fondaparinux,
nadroparin, and heparin.

question: Were efforts made to minimise error in selection of studies?
answers:
Three reviewers (A.O.K., M.A.S., and J.L.D.) independently
evaluated each study for inclusion and exclusion
criteria and tolerability and efficacy end points
and assigned Jadad scores

question: Were efforts made to minimise error in data collection?
answers:
Three reviewers (A.O.K., M.A.S., and J.L.D.) independently evaluated each study for inclusion and exclusion criteria and tolerability and efficacy end points
and assigned Jadad scores. 22,23 We only included RCTs with a corresponding Jadad score of 3 or
greater (maximum score, 5). Discrepancies in Jadad score assignment and inclusion criteria were resolved through consensus and discussion. We abstracted
study characteristics including number of patients, patient age, sex, reason for admission, risk factors for
VTE events, drug, dose, route, schedule, efficacy end
points (VTE, DVT, and PE) and tolerability end points
(major and minor bleeding).

question: Were sufficient study characteristics considered for both review authors and readers to be able to interpret the results?
answers:
The total number of patients included in this analysis
was 12,391, of which 8357 were in placebo-controlled
trials. 1s,26-29 The mean age for the entire
cohort was 72.8 years, with similar distributions in regard
to other baseline characteristics (Tables I and II).
Mean body mass index was 25.6 kg/m 2 and mean
actual body weight was 68.2 kg. Heart failure was reported
more frequently (29.4%) than acute respiratory
disease (22.4%) or infection/inflammation (24.7%)
(Table II). The majority of trials excluded patients
who were mechanically ventilated, had active bleeding,
renal failure, conditions requiring full anticoagulation,
or who were pregnant.

Table I. Characteristics of trials included in the meta-analysis.

question: Were all pre-defined analyses reported or departures explained?
answers: nan

question: Was the synthesis appropriate given the nature and similarity in the research questions, study designs and outcomes across included studies?
answers:
we extracted from each study data to create 3 major comparisons
of efficacy evaluating LMWH/fondaparinux
against placebo, LMWH/fondaparinux against UFH,
or the combination of LMWH/fondaparinux versus
UFH or placebo for the reduction of VTE and the occurrence
of new bleedingData pertaining to each
event were represented as proportions and a metaanalysis
was constructed using Comprehensive Meta
Analysis version 2.2 (Englewood, New Jersey). We
also used the Der Simonian and Laird random-effects
model to calculate pooled odds ratios (ORs) and corresponding
95% CI, with a P value _<0.05 considered
statistically significant. 24 The I2 statistic was used to
estimate variability from heterogeneity in the effect
size of each selected end point. 25 Meta-regression, according
to baseline risk factors, was performed to explore
additional relationships between efficacy and
tolerability end points. 

We clustered dalteparin, enoxaparin, nadroparin, and
fondaparinux together in the LMWH/fondaparinux
group. Fondaparinux was included with LMWH agents
for practicality since fondaparinux has been regarded
as an "ultra-LMWH" and because the pharmacokinetic
and pharmacodynamic properties of fondaparinux
are similar to other LMWH agents, 19 although the
US Food and Drug Administration has recently classified
it as a selective factor Xa inhibitor

answers:

estimate variability from heterogeneity in the effect

question: Were the findings robust, e.g. as demonstrated through funnel plot or sensitivity analyses?
answers: nan

question: Were biases in primary studies minimal or addressed in the synthesis?
answers:
We only included RCTs with a corresponding Jadad score of 3 or
greater (maximum score, 5).

After examining the abstracts
and titles, we selected 14 trials for more detailed
evaluation, of which 9 fulfilled the predefined inclusion
criteria (Table I). ls,26-33 All included reports were RCTs.
The 5 excluded trials included patients who failed to
achieve a minimum Jadad score of 3, mainly due to lack
of randomization.

question: Did the interpretation of findings address all of the concerns identified in Domains 1 to 4?
answers:
Limitations to this meta-analysis should be discussed,
including differences across and within studies.
First, not all studies used the same criteria for VTE
diagnosis or bleeding definitions. In addition, it may
not be ideal to group all LMWHs in 1 drug class because
there are subtle differences between LMWH
agents. We included fondaparinux in the LMWH
group because (1) it has similar pharmacokinetic and
pharmacodynamic characteristics to other LMWH
agents; (2) it is the only selective factor Xa inhibitor
available for comparison; and (3) only 1 trial of fondaparinux
in the medically ill population is available
for comparison. Recognizing these limitations, and in
the absence of other comparator trials, we felt it was
reasonable to combine fondaparinux with other
LMWH agents into 1 group. Also, because the focus
of this analysis was to look at outcomes, the issue of
strict pharmacologic classification was judged to be
less important than the analysis of existing evidence of
efficacy or safety profiles in the routine clinical practice.
Additionally, only English-language studies were
included in this meta-analysis.

Mean body mass index was 25.6 kg/m 2 and mean
actual body weight was 68.2 kg. Heart failure was reported
more frequently (29.4%) than acute respiratory
disease (22.4%) or infection/inflammation (24.7%)
(Table II). The majority of trials excluded patients
who were mechanically ventilated, had active bleeding,
renal failure, conditions requiring full anticoagulation,
or who were pregnant.

Table I. Characteristics of trials included in the meta-analysis.
